title: Re-dispatching tasks on associated processors to reduce lock contention
abstract: A method is disclosed to reduce lock contention in a data storage system. The method dispatches, on a first processor core, a task configured to acquire a lock on a data storage resource, such as memory. The method then determines whether the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource. If the first processor core is not associated with the data storage resource, the method re-dispatches the task on a second processor core that is associated with the data storage resource. In certain embodiments, the task is only re-dispatched on the second processor core if an amount of effort required to acquire the lock is above a selected threshold. If, on the other hand, the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource, the method executes the task on the first processor core. A corresponding system and computer program product are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for reducing lock contention in a data storage system, the method comprising: dispatching, on a first processor core, a task configured to acquire a lock on a data storage resource; determining whether the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource; if the first processor core is not associated with the data storage resource, re-dispatching the task on a second processor core that is associated with the data storage resource; and if the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource, executing the task on the first processor core. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the first processor core is located on a first processor chip of a data storage system. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the second processor core is located on a second processor chip of a data storage system. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the storage resource is a memory. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein re-dispatching the task on the second processor core comprises re dispatching the task on the second processor core only if effort required to acquire the lock is above a selected threshold. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the effort required is measured by at least one of a number of clock cycles needed to acquire the lock, a number of acquisition attempts needed to acquire the lock, and an amount of time needed to acquire the lock. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein acquiring the lock on the data storage resource comprises acquiring the lock to access data on the data storage resource. 8. A computer program product for reducing lock contention in a data storage system, the computer program product comprising a computer-readable medium having computer-usable program code embodied therein, the computer-usable program code configured to perform the following when executed by at least one processor core: dispatch, on a first processor core, a task configured to acquire a lock on a data storage resource; determine whether the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource; if the first processor core is not associated with the data storage resource, re-dispatch the task on a second processor core that is associated with the data storage resource; and if the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource, execute the task on the first 

processor core. 9. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the first processor core is located on a first processor chip of a data storage system. 10. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the second processor core is located on a second processor chip of a data storage system. 11. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the storage resource is a memory. 12. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein re-dispatching the task on the second processor core comprises re-dispatching the task on the second processor core only if effort required to acquire the lock is above a selected threshold. 13. The computer program product of claim 12, wherein the effort required is measured by at least one of a number of clock cycles needed to acquire the lock, a number of acquisition attempts needed to acquire the lock, and an amount of time needed to acquire the lock. 14. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein acquiring the lock on the data storage resource comprises acquiring the lock to access data on the data storage resource. 15. A system for reducing lock contention in a data storage system, the system comprising: a data storage system comprising a first processor core, a second processor core, and a data storage resource, the data storage system configured to: dispatch, on the first processor core, a task configured to acquire a lock on the data storage resource; determine whether the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource; if the first processor core is not associated with the data storage resource, re-dispatch the task on the second processor core that is associated with the data storage resource; and if the first processor core is associated with the data storage resource, execute the task on the first processor core. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the first processor core is located on a first processor chip of the data storage system and the second processor core is located on a second processor chip of the data storage system. 17. The system of claim 15, wherein the storage resource is a memory. 18. The system of claim 15, wherein re-dispatching the task on the second processor core comprises re 

dispatching the task on the second processor core only if effort required to acquire the lock is above a selected threshold. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the effort required is measured by at least one of a number of clock cycles needed to acquire the lock, a number of acquisition attempts needed to acquire the lock, and an amount of time needed to acquire the lock. 20. The system of claim 15, wherein acquiring the lock on the data storage resource comprises acquiring the lock to access data on the data storage resource.
